sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now the centers should be swapped:
- Left agent: c center (was W)
- Right agent: W center (was c)
And the right agent is now active. Let me verify and check if the level completed.
Looking at the image, the left agent now has a dark center (c), and the right agent has a light center (W). The left agent is inside the left c-frame with c center. This matches the c-frame's c border!
But the level didn't complete (level_completed: false). So this configuration alone is not enough.
Let me think about what else might be needed. The right agent is now active and has W center. The right c-frame has a white square inside. Maybe the right agent needs to be in a specific position?
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the goal. What if the goal is:
1. Left agent inside left c-frame with c center (matching the c border) ✓
2. Right agent inside right c-frame with W center (matching the white interior)
But the right agent can't reach the right c-frame because of the purple bar. So this can't be the goal.
Unless... there's another way to get the right agent into the right c-frame. What if I need to move the right agent UP or DOWN to a different row, then move it RIGHT past the purple bar?
The purple bar is at rows 21-41. The panels are at rows 21-41. So the purple bar spans the entire panel height. There's no way to go around it vertically within the panels.
But wait, the gap area is at cols 18-32, rows 21-41. The purple bar is at cols 33-38. What if I move the right agent UP to row 20 or below to row 42, which is outside the panel area? Would the agent be able to move there?
Let me try moving the right agent UP. The right agent is at rows 27-29. Moving UP would put it at rows 26-28. Moving UP again would put it at rows 25-27. Eventually, moving UP enough times would put it at rows 21-23, which is the top edge of the panel. Moving UP further might put it outside the panel.
Let me try moving the right agent UP several times to see if it can exit the panel area.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("=== CURRENT STATE ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 20:
        boundary = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in boundary]
        cols = [p[1] for p in boundary]
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows[{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols[{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], children={n['children']}")

# Right agent is active at rows 27-29, cols 21-23
# Try moving it UP to see if it can exit the panel
# Panel top is at row 21, so agent at rows 27-29 can move UP to rows 24-26 (inside panel)
# Moving UP further might exit the panel
action(['UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
